name: Sourdough English Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Basic sourdough starter, room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Makes 1 dozen muffins
1. In a large bowl, sprinkle with cornmeal and set aside.
2. Dissolve yeast in 1/2 cup lukewarm water in another large bowl and let stand for 5 minutes to proof.
3. Add starter, dry milk, sugar, and salt, then blend well.
4. Mix in remaining water. Gradually add 3 cups flour, beating until smooth.
5. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, adding more flour as needed.
6. Transfer dough to greased bowl, turning to coat all surfaces. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in warm, draft-free area until doubled, about 1-1.5 hours.
7. Sprinkle work surface and baking sheet with cornmeal.
8. Punch down the dough and turn out onto a floured surface. Roll to a thickness of about 1/2 inch.
9. Cut rounds using a 3-inch cutter. Place rounds on the cornmeal side up on the baking sheet. Cover and let rise in warm, draft-free area until almost doubled, about 1 hour.
10. Preheat griddle to 275°F; grease lightly. Cook muffins on both sides until lightly browned, turning once, about 10 minutes per side.
11. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool. Store in an airtight plastic bag. Split and toast just before serving.
